steps.title: Identify the sentence structure
steps.content:
Before the blank: “By linking the Hudson River to Lake Erie” — a participial (introductory) phrase. After the blank: “the canal reduced transport time to nine days and cut costs to six dollars per ton” — a complete independent clause. An introductory participial phrase must be followed by a comma, and nothing else.
  • "Erie; the": A semicolon joins two independent clauses, but the participial phrase before the blank is not an independent clause. ✗
  • "Erie (the": An open parenthesis signals a parenthetical insertion, but the rest of the sentence is the main clause, not a parenthetical. ✗
  • "Erie, the": A comma correctly separates the introductory participial phrase from the independent clause that follows. ✓
  • "Erie: the": A colon introduces a list or elaboration after an independent clause, but the phrase before the blank is not an independent clause. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Rule: introductory participial phrase + comma + independent clause. Only a comma (Erie, the) fits this structure. Every other option requires what comes before the blank to be an independent clause, which it is not.

That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
